evals.prompt: We have a freemium SaaS with 50,000 free users and only 2% convert to paid ($29/month). The paywall appears when users hit feature limits. Our churn on paid is low (4% monthly). How do we improve the free-to-paid conversion?
evals.expected_output: Upgrade conversion strategy covering: paywall trigger optimization, upgrade copy and framing, pricing psychology, in-app upgrade prompts, and email upgrade sequences.
assertions: Addresses paywall trigger timing (hit limit vs. proactive)
Covers upgrade copy — benefit-led vs. feature-led messaging
Addresses pricing psychology at the paywall (loss aversion, anchoring)
Recommends in-app nudges before the hard paywall limit
Notes that low paid churn indicates strong product-market fit — opportunity to be more aggressive with upgrade prompts

evals.prompt: Should we show the paywall before or after users hit the limit?
evals.expected_output: Analysis of reactive vs. proactive paywall placement with recommendation based on user engagement, value demonstration timing, and upgrade intent signals.
assertions: Distinguishes reactive (hit limit) vs. proactive (preview of upgrade value) paywalls
Addresses the goal-gradient effect and timing of upgrade prompts
Recommends a hybrid approach (in-app hints before limit + hard paywall at limit)
Covers how to use usage milestones as upgrade trigger signals

evals.expected_output: Offers paywall/upgrade-focused input on pricing page design, redirects to page-cro or copywriting skill for the full pricing page.
assertions: Offers paywall-specific input (upgrade CTAs, tier presentation)
Redirects to page-cro or copywriting for full pricing page setup
